title: Exceptions should provide standard constructors
description: Exceptions should provide standard constructorsAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoExceptions types should provide the following constructors:public MyException()public MyException(string)public MyException(string, Exception)The absence of these constructors can complicate exception handling and limit the information that can be provided when an exception is thrown.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Exception classes should provide standard constructors: (), (string), and (string, Exception)

logic.reasoning: Uses a custom function to detect exception classes (inheriting from Exception) that don't provide the three standard constructors. Function checks for constructor signatures more accurately than regex and excludes test classes.

should_trigger: public class MyException : Exception { public MyException(string message, int code) : base(message) { } }
public class MissingParameterlessException : Exception { public MissingParameterlessException(string message) : base(message) { } }
should_not_trigger: public class CompliantException : Exception { public CompliantException() { } public CompliantException(string message) : base(message) { } public CompliantException(string message, Exception inner) : base(message, inner) { } }

logic.self_critique_notes: Custom function provides accurate detection of missing standard constructors in exception classes. Handles various edge cases including abstract/sealed classes, test exclusions, and different constructor orderings. Significantly more reliable than complex regex patterns.
